input.description: Explore project structure and registry system
input.prompt:
I need to understand the overall structure of a shadcn/ui component library project. Please explore:

1. The root directory structure (top-level files and folders)
2. How the registry system works - look for registry files, JSON configs, or TypeScript registry definitions
3. How components are registered - find the registry index files
4. The directory structure for components (where .tsx files live)
5. How demo/example files are structured and where they live

Key files to find and read:
- Any registry/*.json or registry/*.ts files
- package.json to understand the project type
- Any index files that list components
- The general folder layout

Be thorough - read the actual file contents of registry files, not just list their names. This is critical to understanding how to add new entries.

1	import { registryItemSchema, type Registry } from "shadcn/schema"
2	import { z } from "zod"
3	
4	import { themes } from "../_legacy-themes"
5	import { blocks } from "./blocks/_registry"
6	import { charts } from "./charts/_registry"
7	import { examples } from "./examples/_registry"
8	import { hooks } from "./hooks/_registry"
9	import { internal } from "./internal/_registry"
10	import { lib } from "./lib/_registry"
11	import { ui } from "./ui/_registry"
12	
13	const DEPRECATED_ITEMS = [
14	  "toast",
15	  "toast-demo",
16	  "toast-destructive",
17	  "toast-simple",
18	  "toast-with-action",
19	  "toast-with-title",
20	]
21	
22	// Shared between index and style for backward compatibility.
23	const NEW_YORK_V4_STYLE = {
24	  type: "registry:style",
25	  dependencies: ["class-variance-authority", "lucide-react", "radix-ui"],
26	  devDependencies: ["tw-animate-css", "shadcn"],
27	  registryDependencies: ["utils"],
28	  css: {
29	    '@import "tw-animate-css"': {},
30	    '@import "shadcn/tailwind.css"': {},
31	    "@layer base": {
32	      "*": {
33	        "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {},
34	      },
35	      body: {
36	        "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {},
37	      },
38	    },
39	  },
40	  cssVars: {},
41	  files: [],
42	}
43	
44	export const registry = {
45	  name: "shadcn/ui",
46	  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
47	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse(
48	    [
49	      {
50	        name: "index",
51	        ...NEW_YORK_V4_STYLE,
52	      },
53	      {
54	        name: "style",
55	        ...NEW_YORK_V4_STYLE,
56	      },
57	      ...ui,
58	      ...blocks,
59	      ...charts,
60	      ...lib,
61	      ...hooks,
62	      ...themes,
63	      ...examples,
64	      ...internal,
65	    ].filter((item) => {
66	      return !DEPRECATED_ITEMS.includes(item.name)
67	    })
68	  ),
69	} satisfies Registry
70

1	import {
2	  iconLibraries,
3	  type IconLibrary,
4	  type IconLibraryName,
5	} from "shadcn/icons"
6	import { z } from "zod"
7	
8	import { BASE_COLORS, type BaseColor } from "@/registry/base-colors"
9	import { BASES, type Base } from "@/registry/bases"
10	import { bodyFonts, fonts, headingFonts } from "@/registry/fonts"
11	import { STYLES, type Style } from "@/registry/styles"
12	import { THEMES, type Theme } from "@/registry/themes"
13	
14	const SHADCN_VERSION = "latest"
15	
16	export { BASES, type Base }
17	export { STYLES, type Style }
18	export { THEMES, type Theme }
19	export { BASE_COLORS, type BaseColor }
20	export { bodyFonts, headingFonts, fonts }
21	export { iconLibraries, type IconLibrary, type IconLibraryName }
22	
23	export type BaseName = Base["name"]
24	export type StyleName = Style["name"]
25	export type ThemeName = Theme["name"]
26	export type BaseColorName = BaseColor["name"]
27	export type ChartColorName = Theme["name"]
28	
29	// Derive font values from registry fonts (e.g., "font-inter" -> "inter").
30	const fontValues = bodyFonts.map((f) => f.name.replace("font-", "")) as [
31	  string,
32	  ...string[],
33	]
34	const fontHeadingValues = ["inherit", ...fontValues] as const
35	
36	export type FontValue = (typeof fontValues)[number]
37	export type FontHeadingValue = (typeof fontHeadingValues)[number]
38	
39	export function getBodyFont(font: FontValue) {
40	  return bodyFonts.find((item) => item.name === `font-${font}`)
41	}
42	
43	export function getHeadingFont(
44	  fontHeading: Exclude<FontHeadingValue, "inherit">
45	) {
46	  return headingFonts.find(
47	    (item) => item.name === `font-heading-${fontHeading}`
48	  )
49	}
50	
51	export function getInheritedHeadingFontValue(font: FontValue) {
52	  return `var(${getBodyFont(font)?.font.variable ?? "--font-sans"})`
53	}
54	
55	export const MENU_ACCENTS = [
56	  { value: "subtle", label: "Subtle" },
57	  { value: "bold", label: "Bold" },
58	] as const
59	
60	export type MenuAccent = (typeof MENU_ACCENTS)[number]
61	export type MenuAccentValue = MenuAccent["value"]
62	
63	export const MENU_COLORS = [
64	  { value: "default", label: "Default" },
65	  { value: "inverted", label: "Inverted" },
66	  { value: "default-translucent", label: "Default Translucent" },
67	  { value: "inverted-translucent", label: "Inverted Translucent" },
68	] as const
69	
70	export type MenuColor = (typeof MENU_COLORS)[number]
71	
72	export type MenuColorValue = MenuColor["value"]
73	
74	export const RADII = [
75	  { name: "default", label: "Default", value: "" },
76	  { name: "none", label: "None", value: "0" },
77	  { name: "small", label: "Small", value: "0.45rem" },
78	  { name: "medium", label: "Medium", value: "0.625rem" },
79	  { name: "large", label: "Large", value: "0.875rem" },
80	] as const
81	
82	export type Radius = (typeof RADII)[number]
83	
84	export type RadiusValue = Radius["name"]
85	
86	export const designSystemConfigSchema = z
87	  .object({
88	    base: z.enum(BASES.map((b) => b.name) as [BaseName, ...BaseName[]]),
89	    style: z.enum(STYLES.map((s) => s.name) as [StyleName, ...StyleName[]]),
90	    iconLibrary: z.enum(
91	      Object.keys(iconLibraries) as [IconLibraryName, ...IconLibraryName[]]

407	export function getThemesForBaseColor(baseColorName: string) {
408	  const baseColorNames = BASE_COLORS.map((bc) => bc.name)
409	
410	  return THEMES.filter((theme) => {
411	    if (theme.name === baseColorName) {
412	      return true
413	    }
414	    return !baseColorNames.includes(theme.name)
415	  })
416	}
417	
418	export function getBase(name: BaseName) {
419	  return BASES.find((base) => base.name === name)
420	}
421	
422	export function getStyle(name: StyleName) {
423	  return STYLES.find((style) => style.name === name)
424	}
425	
426	export function getTheme(name: ThemeName) {
427	  return THEMES.find((theme) => theme.name === name)
428	}
429	
430	export function getBaseColor(name: BaseColorName) {
431	  return BASE_COLORS.find((color) => color.name === name)
432	}
433	
434	export function getIconLibrary(name: IconLibraryName) {
435	  return iconLibraries[name]
436	}
437	
438	// Builds a registry:theme item from a design system config.
439	export function buildRegistryTheme(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
440	  const baseColor = getBaseColor(config.baseColor)
441	  const theme = getTheme(config.theme)
442	
443	  if (!baseColor || !theme) {
444	    throw new Error(
445	      `Base color "${config.baseColor}" or theme "${config.theme}" not found`
446	    )
447	  }
448	
449	  // Merge base color and theme CSS vars.
450	  const lightVars: Record<string, string> = {
451	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
452	    ...(theme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>),
453	  }
454	  const darkVars: Record<string, string> = {
455	    ...(baseColor.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
456	    ...(theme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>),
457	  }
458	  const themeVars: Record<string, string> = {}
459	
460	  // Apply chart color override.
461	  const chartTheme = getTheme(config.chartColor)
462	  if (chartTheme) {
463	    const chartLight = chartTheme.cssVars?.light as Record<string, string>
464	    const chartDark = chartTheme.cssVars?.dark as Record<string, string>
465	    for (let i = 1; i <= 5; i++) {
466	      const key = `chart-${i}`
467	      if (chartLight?.[key]) lightVars[key] = chartLight[key]
468	      if (chartDark?.[key]) darkVars[key] = chartDark[key]
469	    }
470	  }
471	
472	  // Apply menu accent transformation.
473	  if (config.menuAccent === "bold") {
474	    lightVars.accent = lightVars.primary
475	    lightVars["accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
476	    darkVars.accent = darkVars.primary
477	    darkVars["accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
478	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent"] = lightVars.primary
479	    // lightVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = lightVars["primary-foreground"]
480	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent"] = darkVars.primary
481	    // darkVars["sidebar-accent-foreground"] = darkVars["primary-foreground"]
482	  }
483	
484	  // Apply radius transformation.
485	  if (config.radius && config.radius !== "default") {
486	    const radius = RADII.find((r) => r.name === config.radius)
487	    if (radius && radius.value) {
488	      lightVars.radius = radius.value
489	    }
490	  }
491	
492	  return {
493	    name: `${config.baseColor}-${config.theme}`,
494	    type: "registry:theme" as const,
495	    cssVars: {
496	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
497	      light: lightVars,
498	      dark: darkVars,
499	    },
500	  }
501	}
502	
503	// Builds a registry:base item from a design system config.
504	export function buildRegistryBase(config: DesignSystemConfig) {
505	  const baseItem = getBase(config.base)
506	  const iconLibraryItem = getIconLibrary(config.iconLibrary)
507	  const normalizedFontHeading =
508	    config.fontHeading === config.font ? "inherit" : config.fontHeading
509	
510	  if (!baseItem || !iconLibraryItem) {
511	    throw new Error(
512	      `Base "${config.base}" or icon library "${config.iconLibrary}" not found`
513	    )
514	  }
515	
516	  const registryTheme = buildRegistryTheme(config)
517	
518	  // Build dependencies.
519	  const dependencies = [
520	    `shadcn@${SHADCN_VERSION}`,
521	    "class-variance-authority",
522	    "tw-animate-css",
523	    ...(baseItem.dependencies ?? []),
524	    ...iconLibraryItem.packages,
525	  ]
526	
527	  const registryDependencies = ["utils"]
528	  const themeVars = {
529	    ...(registryTheme.cssVars?.theme ?? {}),
530	    ...(normalizedFontHeading === "inherit"
531	      ? { "--font-heading": getInheritedHeadingFontValue(config.font) }
532	      : {}),
533	  }
534	
535	  if (config.font) {
536	    registryDependencies.push(`font-${config.font}`)
537	  }
538	
539	  if (normalizedFontHeading !== "inherit") {
540	    registryDependencies.push(`font-heading-${normalizedFontHeading}`)
541	  }
542	
543	  return {
544	    name: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
545	    extends: "none",
546	    type: "registry:base" as const,
547	    config: {
548	      style: `${config.base}-${config.style}`,
549	      iconLibrary: iconLibraryItem.name,
550	      rtl: config.rtl,
551	      menuColor: config.menuColor,
552	      menuAccent: config.menuAccent,
553	      tailwind: {
554	        baseColor: config.baseColor,
555	      },
556	    },
557	    dependencies,
558	    registryDependencies,
559	    cssVars: {
560	      ...registryTheme.cssVars,
561	      theme: Object.keys(themeVars).length > 0 ? themeVars : undefined,
562	    },
563	    css: {
564	      '@import "tw-animate-css"': {},
565	      '@import "shadcn/tailwind.css"': {},
566	      "@layer base": {
567	        "*": { "@apply border-border outline-ring/50": {} },
568	        body: { "@apply bg-background text-foreground": {} },
569	      },
570	    },
571	    ...(config.rtl && {
572	      docs: `To learn how to set up the RTL provider and fonts for your app, see https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/rtl/${config.template === "next-monorepo" ? "next" : (config.template ?? "next")}`,
573	    }),
574	  }
575	}

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Shadcn/UI Component Library Structure - Complete Overview

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library project. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of its structure:

### 1. ROOT DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Top-level organization:**
```
/
├── apps/
│   └── v4/                    # Main Next.js documentation app
├── packages/
│   ├── shadcn/                # CLI tool package
│   └── tests/                 # Test fixtures
├── templates/                 # Project templates
├── scripts/                   # Build and automation scripts
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml        # Monorepo config
├── package.json               # Root workspace config
└── turbo.json                 # Turborepo config
```

**Key root files:**
- `/package.json` - Workspace configuration with pnpm workspaces
- `/apps/v4/package.json` - Documentation app with build scripts
- `/apps/v4/components.json` - Component configuration (aliases, style, iconLibrary)

### 2. REGISTRY SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

The registry system operates in **two layers**:

#### Layer 1: Authored Source (Version Controlled)
Located at `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/`:
- **base/** - Base UI components built on Base UI library
- **radix/** - Base UI components built on Radix UI library

Each base contains:
```
bases/{base}/
├── ui/                  # UI components (accordion.tsx, button.tsx, etc.)
├── blocks/              # Complex blocks (dashboard-01, login-01, etc.)
├── examples/            # Demo/example files
├── hooks/               # Custom React hooks (use-mobile.ts)
├── lib/                 # Utility libraries (utils.ts)
├── internal/            # Internal components not exposed
├── components/          # Template components (example.tsx)
└── registry.ts          # Assembles all items into registry object
```

#### Layer 2: Generated Styled Variants
Located at `/apps/v4/styles/{base}-{style}/`:
- **Generated during build** by `build-registry.mts` script
- Contains compiled UI components with specific styling applied
- Examples: `base-nova`, `radix-vega`, `base-luma`, etc.
- Also generates `ui-rtl/` variants for Nova styles

### 3. REGISTRY DEFINITION FILES

**Key registry configuration files:**

1. **`/apps/v4/registry/config.ts`** - Main registry configuration
   - Exports BASES, STYLES, THEMES, BASE_COLORS, FONTS
   - Defines `designSystemConfigSchema` (Zod validation)
   - Contains DEFAULT_CONFIG and PRESETS
   - Export functions: `getBase()`, `getStyle()`, `getTheme()`, `buildRegistryTheme()`, `buildRegistryBase()`

2. **`/apps/v4/registry/bases.ts`** - Defines available bases
   - BASES array: `[{name: "radix", ...}, {name: "base", ...}]`
   - Each base has title, description, dependencies, meta (logo)

3. **`/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts`** (Legacy style)
   - Assembles items from multiple sources: `ui`, `blocks`, `charts`, `lib`, `hooks`, `themes`, `examples`, `internal`
   - Filters deprecated items

4. **Base registries** (Source of truth):
   - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/registry.ts`
   - `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/registry.ts`
   - Combine: ui, examples, lib, components, blocks, hooks, internal, fonts

### 4. COMPONENT REGISTRATION - DETAILED BREAKDOWN

**UI Components Registry** (`ui/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
export const ui: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "accordion",
    type: "registry:ui",
    dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
    files: [{
      path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    }],
  },
  // ... more UI components
]
```

**Blocks Registry** (`blocks/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
export const blocks: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "dashboard-01",
    type: "registry:block",
    description: "A dashboard with sidebar, charts and data table.",
    dependencies: ["@dnd-kit/core", ...],
    registryDependencies: ["sidebar", "breadcrumb", ...],
    files: [
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/page.tsx",
        type: "registry:page",
        target: "app/dashboard/page.tsx",  // Where it installs
      },
      {
        path: "blocks/dashboard-01/components/app-sidebar.tsx",
        type: "registry:component",
      },
      // ... nested components
    ],
  },
]
```

**Examples Registry** (`examples/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
export const examples: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "accordion-demo",
    type: "registry:example",
    registryDependencies: ["accordion"],
    files: [{
      path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    }],
  },
]
```

**Hooks Registry** (`hooks/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
export const hooks: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "use-mobile",
    type: "registry:hook",
    files: [{
      path: "hooks/use-mobile.ts",
      type: "registry:hook",
    }],
  },
]
```

**Lib Registry** (`lib/_registry.ts`):
```typescript
export const lib: Registry["items"] = [
  {
    name: "utils",
    type: "registry:lib",
    dependencies: ["clsx", "tailwind-merge"],
    files: [{
      path: "lib/utils.ts",
      type: "registry:lib",
    }],
  },
]
```

### 5. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE FOR COMPONENTS

**Physical file locations:**

```
/apps/v4/
├── registry/
│   ├── bases/
│   │   ├── base/
│   │   │   ├── ui/                          # 57 UI components
│   │   │   ├── blocks/                      # Complex layouts
│   │   │   ├── examples/                    # 62 example demos
│   │   │   ├── hooks/                       # Custom hooks
│   │   │   ├── lib/                         # Utilities
│   │   │   ├── internal/                    # Non-exported items
│   │   │   ├── components/                  # Template components
│   │   │   └── registry.ts
│   │   └── radix/
│   │       └── (same structure as base/)
│   │
│   ├── new-york-v4/                         # Legacy style layer
│   │   ├── ui/                              # 57 UI component files
│   │   ├── blocks/                          # 28 block directories
│   │   │   ├── dashboard-01/
│   │   │   ├── login-01/ through login-05/
│   │   │   ├── signup-01/ through signup-05/
│   │   │   ├── sidebar-01/ through sidebar-16/
│   │   │   └── ...
│   │   ├── examples/                        # 237 demo files
│   │   ├── hooks/
│   │   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── internal/
│   │   ├── charts/
│   │   └── registry.ts
│   │
│   ├── styles/                              # Generated by build
│   │   ├── base-nova/ui/
│   │   ├── base-nova/ui-rtl/
│   │   ├── radix-nova/ui/
│   │   ├── base-vega/ui/
│   │   ├── base-luma/ui/
│   │   └── ... (12 total style variants)
│   │
│   ├── config.ts                            # Configuration & validators
│   ├── bases.ts                             # Base definitions
│   ├── styles.tsx                           # Style definitions
│   ├── themes.ts                            # Theme definitions
│   ├── base-colors.ts                       # Color definitions
│   ├── fonts.ts                             # Font definitions
│   ├── directory.json                       # Directory metadata
│   ├── registry.json                        # Generated flat registry (153KB)
│   └── icons/                               # Icon library definitions
│
└── scripts/
    ├── build-registry.mts                   # Main build pipeline
    ├── build-icons.ts
    ├── build-test-app.mts
    └── validate-registries.mts
```

### 6. DEMO/EXAMPLE FILE STRUCTURE

**Example files live in:**
- `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/` (62 files)
- `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/` (237 files)

**Naming convention:**
- `{component-name}-demo.tsx` - Main demo
- `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx` - Variant example (e.g., `badge-destructive.tsx`)

**Example content:**
```typescript
// examples/accordion-demo.tsx
import { Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion"

export function AccordionDemo() {
  return (
    <Accordion type="single" collapsible>
      <AccordionItem value="item-1">
        <AccordionTrigger>Is it accessible?</AccordionTrigger>
        <AccordionContent>...</AccordionContent>
      </AccordionItem>
    </Accordion>
  )
}
```

**Block structure (complex example):**
```
blocks/dashboard-01/
├── page.tsx                              # Main page
├── data.json                             # Static data
└── components/
    ├── app-sidebar.tsx
    ├── chart-area-interactive.tsx
    ├── data-table.tsx
    ├── nav-documents.tsx
    ├── nav-main.tsx
    ├── nav-secondary.tsx
    ├── nav-user.tsx
    └── section-cards.tsx
```

### 7. BUILD PIPELINE

**Build script:** `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts`

**Execution order:**
1. Build `registry/bases/__index__.tsx` from authored base registries
2. Build temporary styled registries (registry/{base-style}/*)
3. Build `registry/__index__.tsx` for runtime lookup
4. Build examples/__index__.tsx from authored demos
5. Export public/r/* for every style via shadcn CLI
6. Copy compiled ui/* from temporary registries into styles/{style}/ui
7. Build ui-rtl/* for base-nova and radix-nova only
8. Format generated outputs
9. Clean up temporary files

**Style combinations generated:** BASES × STYLES
- BASES: [base, radix] (2)
- STYLES: [vega, nova, maia, lyra, luma, mira] (6)
- Total: 12 style variants

### 8. CONFIGURATION FILES

**Components.json** (`/apps/v4/components.json`):
```json
{
  "style": "new-york",
  "rsc": true,
  "tsx": true,
  "tailwind": {
    "baseColor": "neutral",
    "cssVariables": true
  },
  "aliases": {
    "components": "@/components",
    "ui": "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui",
    "lib": "@/lib",
    "hooks": "@/hooks"
  },
  "iconLibrary": "lucide"
}
```

**Registry JSON** (`/apps/v4/registry.json`):
- Generated file containing all registry items in flat structure
- ~153KB file with complete paths and dependencies
- Output of the build pipeline
- Used by CLI for installation

### 9. KEY PATHS SUMMARY

| Purpose | Path |
|---------|------|
| **UI Components** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/ui/*.tsx` |
| **Blocks** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/blocks/*/` |
| **Examples** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/examples/*.tsx` |
| **Hooks** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/hooks/*.ts` |
| **Utilities** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/lib/*.ts` |
| **Registry Definitions** | `/apps/v4/registry/{base}/*/registry.ts` or `_registry.ts` |
| **Generated Styles** | `/apps/v4/styles/{base}-{style}/ui/*.tsx` |
| **Build Config** | `/apps/v4/registry/config.ts`, `bases.ts`, `styles.tsx`, `themes.ts` |
| **Build Pipeline** | `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` |
| **Output Registry** | `/apps/v4/registry.json` |

### 10. REGISTRY ITEM TYPES

Registry items can be one of these types:
- `registry:style` - Style configuration
- `registry:ui` - UI components
- `registry:block` - Complex layout blocks
- `registry:example` - Demo/example files
- `registry:hook` - Custom React hooks
- `registry:lib` - Utility functions
- `registry:component` - Template components
- `registry:page` - Full pages (with target installation path)
- `registry:file` - Static files (data.json, etc.)

This is the complete architecture of the shadcn/ui v4 component library system!

```json
  "iconLibrary": "lucide"
}
```

**Registry JSON** (`/apps/v4/registry.json`):
- Generated file containing all registry items in flat structure
- ~153KB file with complete paths and dependencies
- Output of the build pipeline
- Used by CLI for installation

### 9. KEY PATHS SUMMARY

| Purpose | Path |
|---------|------|
| **UI Components** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/ui/*.tsx` |
| **Blocks** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/blocks/*/` |
| **Examples** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/examples/*.tsx` |
| **Hooks** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/hooks/*.ts` |
| **Utilities** | `/apps/v4/registry/bases/{base}/lib/*.ts` |
| **Registry Definitions** | `/apps/v4/registry/{base}/*/registry.ts` or `_registry.ts` |
| **Generated Styles** | `/apps/v4/styles/{base}-{style}/ui/*.tsx` |
| **Build Config** | `/apps/v4/registry/config.ts`, `bases.ts`, `styles.tsx`, `themes.ts` |
| **Build Pipeline** | `/apps/v4/scripts/build-registry.mts` |
| **Output Registry** | `/apps/v4/registry.json` |

### 10. REGISTRY ITEM TYPES

Registry items can be one of these types:
- `registry:style` - Style configuration
- `registry:ui` - UI components
- `registry:block` - Complex layout blocks
- `registry:example` - Demo/example files
- `registry:hook` - Custom React hooks
- `registry:lib` - Utility functions
- `registry:component` - Template components
- `registry:page` - Full pages (with target installation path)
- `registry:file` - Static files (data.json, etc.)

This is the complete architecture of the shadcn/ui v4 component library system!

## File 4: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts`

## File 5: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/progress.tsx`

Full file content (all 80 lines):
- Uses `@base-ui/react` Progress primitives
- Exports: `Progress`, `ProgressTrack`, `ProgressIndicator`, `ProgressLabel`, `ProgressValue`
- Composite pattern with separate subcomponents
- Root component wraps Track and Indicator by default

## File 6: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/pagination.tsx`

Full file content (all 156 lines):
- Plain React implementation (no headless library dependencies)
- Uses semantic HTML: `<nav>`, `<ul>`, `<li>`, `<a>`
- Exports: `Pagination`, `PaginationContent`, `PaginationItem`, `PaginationLink`, `PaginationPrevious`, `PaginationNext`, `PaginationEllipsis`
- Composite pattern with specialized subcomponents (Previous, Next, Ellipsis)
- Uses `Button` component internally and `IconPlaceholder` for icons

## File 7: `/apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/progress.tsx`

Full file content (all 31 lines):
- Uses `radix-ui` Progress primitive
- Simpler than base version - only exports `Progress` component
- Single component export (not composite)
- Directly renders `ProgressPrimitive.Root` with nested `ProgressPrimitive.Indicator`

## Key Observations for Stepper Implementation:

1. **Registry Entry Pattern**: Simple object with `name`, `type: "registry:ui"`, `files` array, optional `registryDependencies` and `dependencies`, and `meta` with docs links
2. **Composite Component Pattern**: Like pagination, stepper can use a parent component with exported subcomponents (Stepper, StepperItem, StepperContent, etc.)
3. **No Library Dependency Required**: Pagination shows you can build components without headless UI libraries - pure React with HTML
4. **Context-Based State Management**: For stepper, you'd likely use React Context to manage current step state across subcomponents (similar to how Tabs or Accordion work internally)
5. **End-of-Array Format**: Both registries end with a comma after the last object, followed by closing bracket on new line

content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need to create a comprehensive plan. Let me now plan the implementation of the Stepper component.

## Summary of what I've learned:

### Project structure:
- Two base variants: `base` (uses `@base-ui/react`) and `radix` (uses `radix-ui`)
- Each has: `ui/` (components), `examples/` (demo files), and registry files (`_registry.ts`)
- The component and example files are nearly identical between base and radix variants, with only import paths differing
- Components from custom HTML (no headless lib) like `pagination.tsx` use plain React with `cn()` and `data-slot` attributes


- I need to create four new files: the stepper component and example for both base and radix variants, then update the registry files to include entries for these new components.
- Since neither UI library has a native stepper primitive, I'll build this as a custom React component using Context to manage the current step and orientation, with a root `Stepper` component and `StepperItem` sub-components for individual steps.
- The component structure includes `StepperTrigger` for navigation, `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` for content, `StepperSeparator` for visual connectors, and `StepperPanel` for step content similar to tabs.
- I'm designing the API to support both controlled and uncontrolled modes, with the root context providing the active step value and change handler.

Writing component API... I'm thinking through the component architecture for a stepper. The key pieces are a root `Stepper` container that manages state like the active step and orientation, a `StepperList` to hold all the step triggers, individual `StepperItem` wrappers for each step, the `StepperTrigger` as the clickable button showing the step number and info, and `StepperTitle` for the label text. The layout can be horizontal with separators between steps and content below, or vertical with separators connecting steps and content at the bottom. I'm reconsidering the structure — the `StepperSeparator` should sit between `StepperItem` components in the list, not as a separate concern. Let me sketch out how this would actually look in practice with the proper nesting and component hierarchy.

Now I'm defining the context structure with a root `StepperContextValue` that tracks the active step and orientation, plus a per-item context for managing individual step state like completion and disabled status. The implementation plan involves building out the `Stepper` component...

Building the component structure... value?: string` - Controlled active value
- `defaultValue?: string` - Uncontrolled default
- `onValueChange?: (value: string) => void` - Callback
- `orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"` - Layout direction
- Uses `React.useState` internally for uncontrolled mode

The `StepperList` component:
- Wrapper `<div>` or `<ol>` for the list of steps
- Flexbox: horizontal = `flex-row`, vertical = `flex-col`

The `StepperItem` component:
- `value: string` - Required, the step's identifier
- `completed?: boolean` - Whether this step is done
- `disabled?: boolean` - Whether this step is disabled
- Provides per-item context

The `StepperTrigger` component:
- `<button>` element
- Gets current step state from parent contexts
- Sets `data-active`, `data-completed`, `data-disabled` attributes based on state and triggers `onValueChange` when clicked

The `StepperTitle` and `StepperDescription` components are simple text wrappers, with the description being smaller and muted. The `StepperSeparator` is a visual connector that adapts to the orientation, and the `StepperPanel` shows or hides content based on which step is active, similar to how tab panels work.

For the `data-slot` naming convention, I'm mapping each component to its slot name: `Stepper` gets `data-slot="stepper"`, `StepperList` gets `data-slot="stepper-list"`, and `StepperItem` gets `data-slot="stepper-item"`. Writing component styles... Writing component styles...

I'm thinking through how to handle the vertical layout—the separator needs different dimensions and positioning, and I should follow the same `data-orientation` pattern that the Tabs component uses with group selectors. Let me map out the exact file structure and implementation details. So the constraint about not modifying existing component files is actually fine—I'm only appending registry entries to the `_registry.ts` files, which is allowed. Now I'm setting up the stepper component with a context-based architecture to manage state across the component hierarchy.

Building the component structure... Writing component structure... Writing component styles... Writing component definitions... I'm setting up example components that showcase different stepper configurations, starting with a basic version that defaults to the second step and then building out variations with descriptions, vertical orientation, and controlled state management. I'm realizing the separator placement needs adjustment—it should sit between items as a sibling rather than nested inside them, which is more consistent with how other UI libraries structure steppers. Let me restructure the `StepperList` to handle this layout properly.

Building the layout structure...

I think the `StepperTrigger` should let users compose their own content rather than forcing a specific structure—they can add a badge for the step number, then the title and description. For vertical layouts, I'll rely on Tailwind utilities to position the indicator on the left with the text stacked to the right, keeping the component flexible while providing sensible defaults through the `cn-` classes. I'm working through the layout structure for the stepper component—the `StepperList` needs different flex directions depending on orientation, while `StepperItem` and `StepperSeparator` need to position themselves appropriately for horizontal versus vertical layouts, with the separator placement being the trickier part to get right.

Since this library uses style-specific `cn-` classes for visual design, I should focus the component on providing the structural CSS and data attributes while letting the style files handle the actual appearance.

Building the component structure... I'm working through the Tailwind classes for each orientation—horizontal uses flexbox with row direction and centered items, while vertical stacks items in a column with separators positioned between them. The vertical layout is trickier since the separator needs to align with the step indicator, so I'm considering whether to use absolute positioning or nest the separator within each item itself.

Looking at how other libraries solve this, I realize the cleanest approach is to keep separators as separate elements but position them intelligently—for vertical layouts, I'll use a left margin that assumes a standard indicator size, with the flexibility for users to override via className if needed. This balances simplicity with customization.

Now I'm settling on the final component structure and documenting the implementation plan.

I need to create the stepper component in both base and radix registries, along with examples for each, then update the registry files to include these new entries.

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me write the implementation plan.

Key observations:
1. The stepper needs to be built for BOTH `base` and `radix` variants (they have almost identical files)
2. Since there's no native Stepper primitive in @base-ui/react or radix-ui, I'll build it as a pure React compound component using React Context (like pagination.tsx does it but more complex since we need state sharing)
3. The only difference between base and radix variants is the import path for `cn` and components


4. I need to create seven sub-components: a root Stepper that manages orientation and active step state, individual StepperItems for each step, clickable StepperTriggers for navigation, StepperTitle and StepperDescription for text content, StepperSeparators for visual dividers, and StepperContent panels that display conditionally based on the active step.

Now I'm setting up the React Context structure with interfaces for both the main Stepper context (tracking active step, orientation, and change handlers) and the StepperItem context (tracking individual step numbers and disabled states). I'll need to create the component files across both base and Radix variants, plus update the registry to include the new stepper component. Setting up the context hooks and establishing the compound component structure with controlled/uncontrolled state management, then defining how individual step items will receive and use their step number and disabled state through context.

Now I'm thinking through the component API and how each piece fits together—the trigger button will handle step changes, the content will conditionally render based on the active step, and I'll add data attributes to track the active, completed, and disabled states for styling purposes.

Sketching out the usage pattern... I'm reconsidering the structure here—there are two common patterns for steppers, and I need to figure out which one makes more sense. The first embeds content directly inside each step item, while the second keeps the step indicators separate from the content sections that come after. Let me think through which approach works better...

For a vertical layout, the content should appear right below each trigger, but for horizontal, the content panels would sit below the entire step indicator row. I'm leaning toward the embedded pattern since it keeps everything self-contained and the StepperContent can access the step context directly.

The cleanest approach is having StepperContent check its step prop against the active step from context and render conditionally, regardless of where it's placed inside the Stepper root. This gives users flexibility in how they structure their markup.

Building out the full horizontal example... I'm realizing that `StepperContent` needs flexible context handling — when it's nested inside a `StepperItem`, it can pull the step value from context, but when it stands alone, the step prop becomes required. Let me finalize the component API with this in mind, making sure `StepperContent` handles both cases gracefully. Writing the controlled component logic... I'm realizing the orientation affects how the entire layout flows—for horizontal steppers, the header row stacks above the content vertically, while for vertical steppers, each step and its content flow together in a column. The key is that the root container should always be flex-col, with the stepper header itself being flex-row for horizontal orientation, and the separators need to adapt their direction based on whether steps are arranged horizontally or vertically.

Writing component styles... I'm realizing the compound component pattern gives users flexibility to compose the indicator, title, and description themselves inside the `StepperTrigger`, rather than having the component enforce a specific structure — this aligns with shadcn/ui's philosophy of being unopinionated about layout.

Now I need to map out the files I'm creating: the stepper component itself for both base and radix variants, example files showing how to use it, and updates to the registry files to register these new components.

# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component

## Context

The codebase is a shadcn/ui v4 monorepo (`apps/v4/`) with two parallel base variants — `base` (built on `@base-ui/react`) and `radix` (built on `radix-ui`). Every UI component and example must be added to **both** variants. Neither `@base-ui/react` nor `radix-ui` exposes a native Stepper primitive, so this component is built entirely with React Context and plain HTML elements — the same approach used by `pagination.tsx`.

The component must include:
- `Stepper` — root container with orientation + controlled/uncontrolled active-step state via Context
- `StepperItem` — wraps one step; propagates its `step` number and `disabled` flag via a second nested Context
- `StepperTrigger` — `<button>` that fires `onValueChange(step)`; reads step from `StepperItemContext`
- `StepperTitle` — styled heading text inside a trigger
- `StepperDescription` — styled secondary text inside a trigger
- `StepperSeparator` — visual line between steps; adapts direction via parent orientation data-attribute
- `StepperContent` — panel shown only when its step is active; step inferred from `StepperItemContext` or overridden by explicit `step` prop

---

## Files to Create (6 new files)

| # | Path | Purpose |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` | Base variant component |
| 2 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` | Radix variant (same code, different `cn` import path) |
| 3 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Base demo |
| 4 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Radix demo (same code, different import paths) |

## Files to Modify (4 registry files — append only, no existing entries touched)

| # | Path | Change |
|---|------|--------|
| 5 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper` entry before closing `]` |
| 6 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper` entry before closing `]` |
| 7 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper-example` entry before closing `]` |
| 8 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper-example` entry before closing `]` |

---

## Step 1 — `stepper.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx`

The radix variant (`apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`) is **identical** except the `cn` import path changes from `@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils` to `@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils`.

### Architecture

Two React Contexts:

```typescript
// Root context — orientation + active step state
StepperContext: { value: number; onValueChange: (v: number) => void; orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" }

// Per-item context — step index + disabled flag
StepperItemContext: { step: number; disabled: boolean }
```

### Controlled/Uncontrolled pattern

```typescript
function Stepper({ value: valueProp, defaultValue = 1, onValueChange, orientation = "horizontal", ... }) {
  const [uncontrolled, setUncontrolled] = React.useState(defaultValue)
  const active = valueProp ?? uncontrolled
  const handleChange = (step: number) => {
    if (valueProp === undefined) setUncontrolled(step)
    onValueChange?.(step)
  }
  // Provide context + render <div data-slot="stepper" data-orientation={orientation}>
}
```

### Sub-components summary

```typescript
// StepperItem — reads orientation via CSS group, sets item context
// Props: step: number, disabled?: boolean
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-item" data-step={step} data-active={active===step}
//               data-completed={active>step} data-disabled={disabled}>
//   data-orientation flows down from group/stepper via CSS

// StepperTrigger — reads step+disabled from StepperItemContext
// Renders: <button data-slot="stepper-trigger" disabled={disabled}
//              onClick={() => !disabled && onValueChange(step)}>

// StepperTitle
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-title">

// StepperDescription
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-description">

// StepperSeparator
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-separator" aria-hidden>
//   Orientation-aware via group-data-horizontal/stepper vs group-data-vertical/stepper CSS

// StepperContent — reads step from StepperItemContext (falls back to explicit prop)
// Only renders children when step === active
// Props: step?: number
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-content" hidden={!isActive}>
```

### Full className scheme (follows `cn-` prefix convention)

```
Stepper:          "cn-stepper group/stepper flex flex-col"
StepperItem:      "cn-stepper-item group/stepper-item flex items-center
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-row group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-col"
StepperTrigger:   "cn-stepper-trigger flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-sm
                   text-left transition-colors outline-none
                   focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
                   disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"
StepperTitle:     "cn-stepper-title text-sm font-medium leading-none"
StepperDescription:"cn-stepper-description text-sm text-muted-foreground"
StepperSeparator: "cn-stepper-separator shrink-0 bg-border
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:h-px group-data-horizontal/stepper:w-full group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-1
                   group-data-vertical/stepper:h-full group-data-vertical/stepper:w-px group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-1 group-data-vertical/stepper:self-stretch"
StepperContent:   "cn-stepper-content"  (hidden when not active)
```

### Exports

```typescript
export {
  Stepper,
  StepperItem,
  StepperTrigger,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperContent,
}
```

---

## Step 2 — `stepper-example.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

The radix variant is identical except all import paths change from `bases/base` to `bases/radix`.

The file exports a default `StepperExample` function wrapping all sub-examples in `<ExampleWrapper>`.

### Sub-examples (each in its own named function)

| Function | Demonstrates |
|----------|-------------|
| `StepperBasic` | Horizontal, uncontrolled, 3 steps with titles only |
| `StepperWithDescriptions` | Horizontal, with title + description per step |
| `StepperVertical` | `orientation="vertical"` with `StepperContent` panels inside each item |
| `StepperControlled` | `"use client"`, `useState`, controlled `value`/`onValueChange`, Next/Back buttons |
| `StepperWithDisabled` | One step marked `disabled` |

### Import pattern (follows pagination-example.tsx conventions)
```typescript
"use client"   // at file top — needed for useState in StepperControlled

import { Example, ExampleWrapper } from "@/registry/bases/base/components/example"
import {
  Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper"
import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/button"
```

---

## Step 3 — Registry entry for `stepper` UI component

Append to the end of the `ui` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      },
    ],
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/base/stepper",
        examples:
          "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx",
      },
    },
  },
```

For the **radix** `_registry.ts`, change the URL paths from `base` → `radix`.

---

## Step 4 — Registry entry for `stepper-example`

Append to the end of the `examples` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** example registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper-example",
    title: "Stepper",
    type: "registry:example",
    registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "example"],
    files: [
      {
        path: "examples/stepper-example.tsx",
        type: "registry:example",
      },
    ],
  },
```

---

## Key Patterns to Follow (cross-check before implementation)

- `"use client"` at the top of all component and example files
- All sub-components use `data-slot="stepper-*"` attributes
- All `className` props start with a `cn-*` semantic class then Tailwind utilities, merged via `cn()`
- Props are typed as `React.ComponentProps<"element"> & { extra props }` — no external type imports needed
- Orientation forwarded via `data-orientation` on root and consumed in children via `group-data-horizontal/stepper:*` / `group-data-vertical/stepper:*` CSS selectors
- Both `base` and `radix` variants get identical implementation (only import path differs: `bases/base` vs `bases/radix`)
- Example file naming: `stepper-example.tsx` (not `stepper-demo.tsx`)
- Example sub-functions are not exported (only the default export is)
- No modifications to any existing files other than appending to the four `_registry.ts` arrays

---

## Verification

1. **TypeScript** — Run `pnpm --filter @v4/app typecheck` (or equivalent) and confirm zero new errors
2. **Visual** — Open the dev server (`pnpm dev` in `apps/v4`) and navigate to the stepper example page to verify all 5 sub-examples render correctly in both horizontal and vertical orientations
3. **Registry validation** — Run `pnpm --filter @v4/app validate-registries` (or `tsx scripts/validate-registries.mts`) to confirm the new entries parse cleanly against the Zod schema
4. **Manual interaction** — Click each `StepperTrigger` in the Controlled example and verify active step advances; verify disabled step cannot be clicked

## Step 1 — `stepper.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx`

The radix variant (`apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`) is **identical** except the `cn` import path changes from `@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils` to `@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils`.

### Architecture

Two React Contexts:

```typescript
// Root context — orientation + active step state
StepperContext: { value: number; onValueChange: (v: number) => void; orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" }

// Per-item context — step index + disabled flag
StepperItemContext: { step: number; disabled: boolean }
```

### Controlled/Uncontrolled pattern

```typescript
function Stepper({ value: valueProp, defaultValue = 1, onValueChange, orientation = "horizontal", ... }) {
  const [uncontrolled, setUncontrolled] = React.useState(defaultValue)
  const active = valueProp ?? uncontrolled
  const handleChange = (step: number) => {
    if (valueProp === undefined) setUncontrolled(step)
    onValueChange?.(step)
  }
  // Provide context + render <div data-slot="stepper" data-orientation={orientation}>
}
```

### Sub-components summary

```typescript
// StepperItem — reads orientation via CSS group, sets item context
// Props: step: number, disabled?: boolean
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-item" data-step={step} data-active={active===step}
//               data-completed={active>step} data-disabled={disabled}>
//   data-orientation flows down from group/stepper via CSS

// StepperTrigger — reads step+disabled from StepperItemContext
// Renders: <button data-slot="stepper-trigger" disabled={disabled}
//              onClick={() => !disabled && onValueChange(step)}>

// StepperTitle
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-title">

// StepperDescription
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-description">

// StepperSeparator
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-separator" aria-hidden>
//   Orientation-aware via group-data-horizontal/stepper vs group-data-vertical/stepper CSS

// StepperContent — reads step from StepperItemContext (falls back to explicit prop)
// Only renders children when step === active
// Props: step?: number
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-content" hidden={!isActive}>
```

### Full className scheme (follows `cn-` prefix convention)

```
Stepper:          "cn-stepper group/stepper flex flex-col"
StepperItem:      "cn-stepper-item group/stepper-item flex items-center
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-row group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-col"
StepperTrigger:   "cn-stepper-trigger flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-sm
                   text-left transition-colors outline-none
                   focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
                   disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"
StepperTitle:     "cn-stepper-title text-sm font-medium leading-none"
StepperDescription:"cn-stepper-description text-sm text-muted-foreground"
StepperSeparator: "cn-stepper-separator shrink-0 bg-border
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:h-px group-data-horizontal/stepper:w-full group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-1
                   group-data-vertical/stepper:h-full group-data-vertical/stepper:w-px group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-1 group-data-vertical/stepper:self-stretch"
StepperContent:   "cn-stepper-content"  (hidden when not active)
```

### Exports

```typescript
export {
  Stepper,
  StepperItem,
  StepperTrigger,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperContent,
}
```

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

The radix variant is identical except all import paths change from `bases/base` to `bases/radix`.

| `StepperBasic` | Horizontal, uncontrolled, 3 steps with titles only |
| `StepperWithDescriptions` | Horizontal, with title + description per step |
| `StepperVertical` | `orientation="vertical"` with `StepperContent` panels inside each item |
| `StepperControlled` | `"use client"`, `useState`, controlled `value`/`onValueChange`, Next/Back buttons |
| `StepperWithDisabled` | One step marked `disabled` |

### Import pattern (follows pagination-example.tsx conventions)
```typescript

  Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper"
import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/button"
```

---

## Step 3 — Registry entry for `stepper` UI component

Append to the end of the `ui` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      },
    ],
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/base/stepper",

      },
    },
  },
```

```typescript
        type: "registry:example",
      },
    ],
```

# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component

## Context

The codebase is a shadcn/ui v4 monorepo (`apps/v4/`) with two parallel base variants — `base` (built on `@base-ui/react`) and `radix` (built on `radix-ui`). Every UI component and example must be added to **both** variants. Neither `@base-ui/react` nor `radix-ui` exposes a native Stepper primitive, so this component is built entirely with React Context and plain HTML elements — the same approach used by `pagination.tsx`.

The component must include:
- `Stepper` — root container with orientation + controlled/uncontrolled active-step state via Context
- `StepperItem` — wraps one step; propagates its `step` number and `disabled` flag via a second nested Context
- `StepperTrigger` — `<button>` that fires `onValueChange(step)`; reads step from `StepperItemContext`
- `StepperTitle` — styled heading text inside a trigger

- `StepperSeparator` — visual line between steps; adapts direction via parent orientation data-attribute
- `StepperContent` — panel shown only when its step is active; step inferred from `StepperItemContext` or overridden by explicit `step` prop

```
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-row group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-col"
StepperTrigger:   "cn-stepper-trigger flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-sm
                   text-left transition-colors outline-none
                   focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
                   disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"
StepperTitle:     "cn-stepper-title text-sm font-medium leading-none"
StepperDescription:"cn-stepper-description text-sm text-muted-foreground"
StepperSeparator: "cn-stepper-separator shrink-0 bg-border
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:h-px group-data-horizontal/stepper:w-full group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-1
                   group-data-vertical/stepper:h-full group-data-vertical/stepper:w-px group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-1 group-data-vertical/stepper:self-stretch"
StepperContent:   "cn-stepper-content"  (hidden when not active)
```

### Exports

```typescript
export {
  Stepper,
  StepperItem,
  StepperTrigger,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperContent,
}
```

---

## Step 2 — `stepper-example.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

The radix variant is identical except all import paths change from `bases/base` to `bases/radix`.

The file exports a default `StepperExample` function wrapping all sub-examples in `<ExampleWrapper>`.

### Sub-examples (each in its own named function)

| Function | Demonstrates |
|----------|-------------|
| `StepperBasic` | Horizontal, uncontrolled, 3 steps with titles only |
| `StepperWithDescriptions` | Horizontal, with title + description per step |
| `StepperVertical` | `orientation="vertical"` with `StepperContent` panels inside each item |
| `StepperControlled` | `"use client"`, `useState`, controlled `value`/`onValueChange`, Next/Back buttons |
| `StepperWithDisabled` | One step marked `disabled` |

### Import pattern (follows pagination-example.tsx conventions)
```typescript
"use client"   // at file top — needed for useState in StepperControlled

import { Example, ExampleWrapper } from "@/registry/bases/base/components/example"
import {
  Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper"
import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/button"
```

---

## Step 3 — Registry entry for `stepper` UI component

Append to the end of the `ui` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      },
    ],
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/base/stepper",
        examples:
          "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx",
      },
    },
  },
```

For the **radix** `_registry.ts`, change the URL paths from `base` → `radix`.

---

## Step 4 — Registry entry for `stepper-example`

Append to the end of the `examples` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** example registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper-example",
    title: "Stepper",
    type: "registry:example",
    registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "example"],
    files: [
      {
        path: "examples/stepper-example.tsx",
        type: "registry:example",
      },
    ],
  },
```

---

## Key Patterns to Follow (cross-check before implementation)

- `"use client"` at the top of all component and example files
- All sub-components use `data-slot="stepper-*"` attributes
- All `className` props start with a `cn-*` semantic class then Tailwind utilities, merged via `cn()`
- Props are typed as `React.ComponentProps<"element"> & { extra props }` — no external type imports needed
- Orientation forwarded via `data-orientation` on root and consumed in children via `group-data-horizontal/stepper:*` / `group-data-vertical/stepper:*` CSS selectors
- Both `base` and `radix` variants get identical implementation (only import path differs: `bases/base` vs `bases/radix`)
- Example file naming: `stepper-example.tsx` (not `stepper-demo.tsx`)
- Example sub-functions are not exported (only the default export is)
- No modifications to any existing files other than appending to the four `_registry.ts` arrays

---

## Verification

1. **TypeScript** — Run `pnpm --filter @v4/app typecheck` (or equivalent) and confirm zero new errors
2. **Visual** — Open the dev server (`pnpm dev` in `apps/v4`) and navigate to the stepper example page to verify all 5 sub-examples render correctly in both horizontal and vertical orientations
3. **Registry validation** — Run `pnpm --filter @v4/app validate-registries` (or `tsx scripts/validate-registries.mts`) to confirm the new entries parse cleanly against the Zod schema
4. **Manual interaction** — Click each `StepperTrigger` in the Controlled example and verify active step advances; verify disabled step cannot be clicked

The plan is written to `/Users/timpietrusky/.claude/plans/sleepy-napping-frog.md`. Here's a summary of what will be implemented:

---

## Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component

**8 file changes — 4 new files, 4 registry appends (no existing code modified)**

### What the exploration found

- The repo has **two parallel base variants** (`base` using `@base-ui/react`, `radix` using `radix-ui`) — every component must be added to both. The files are identical except for `cn` import paths.
- Neither library has a native Stepper primitive → built from scratch using **React Context + plain HTML elements**, following the same pattern as `pagination.tsx`.
- Examples are named `*-example.tsx` and registered in `examples/_registry.ts`.

---

### New files (4)

**Component files:**
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx`
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`

Seven exports: `Stepper`, `StepperItem`, `StepperTrigger`, `StepperTitle`, `StepperDescription`, `StepperSeparator`, `StepperContent`

Two React Contexts: `StepperContext` (root state: value, onValueChange, orientation) and `StepperItemContext` (per-step: step index + disabled). Supports controlled + uncontrolled usage. Orientation passed as `data-orientation` and consumed in children via `group-data-horizontal/stepper:*` / `group-data-vertical/stepper:*` CSS group selectors. `StepperItem` renders `data-active`, `data-completed`, `data-disabled` attributes. `StepperContent` hides via `hidden` when its step ≠ active.

**Example files:**
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx`
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

Five sub-examples: Basic, With Descriptions, Vertical (with content panels), Controlled (useState + Next/Back buttons), With Disabled.

---

### Registry appends (4 files — append only, before closing `]`)

- `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` → `stepper` UI entry
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` → `stepper` UI entry
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts` → `stepper-example` entry (`registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "example"]`)
- `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` → same

---

### Verification
1. TypeScript check — no new errors
2. Dev server — all 5 sub-examples render in both orientations
3. Registry validation script — new entries parse against the Zod schema
4. Click test — controlled stepper advances steps; disabled step is not clickable

# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component

## Context

The codebase is a shadcn/ui v4 monorepo (`apps/v4/`) with two parallel base variants — `base` (built on `@base-ui/react`) and `radix` (built on `radix-ui`). Every UI component and example must be added to **both** variants. Neither `@base-ui/react` nor `radix-ui` exposes a native Stepper primitive, so this component is built entirely with React Context and plain HTML elements — the same approach used by `pagination.tsx`.

The component must include:
- `Stepper` — root container with orientation + controlled/uncontrolled active-step state via Context
- `StepperItem` — wraps one step; propagates its `step` number and `disabled` flag via a second nested Context
- `StepperTrigger` — `<button>` that fires `onValueChange(step)`; reads step from `StepperItemContext`
- `StepperTitle` — styled heading text inside a trigger
- `StepperDescription` — styled secondary text inside a trigger
- `StepperSeparator` — visual line between steps; adapts direction via parent orientation data-attribute
- `StepperContent` — panel shown only when its step is active; step inferred from `StepperItemContext` or overridden by explicit `step` prop

---

## Files to Create (6 new files)

| # | Path | Purpose |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` | Base variant component |
| 2 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` | Radix variant (same code, different `cn` import path) |
| 3 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Base demo |
| 4 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Radix demo (same code, different import paths) |

## Files to Modify (4 registry files — append only, no existing entries touched)

| # | Path | Change |
|---|------|--------|
| 5 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper` entry before closing `]` |
| 6 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper` entry before closing `]` |
| 7 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper-example` entry before closing `]` |
| 8 | `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` | Append `stepper-example` entry before closing `]` |

---

## Step 1 — `stepper.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx`

The radix variant (`apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`) is **identical** except the `cn` import path changes from `@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils` to `@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils`.

### Architecture

Two React Contexts:

```typescript
// Root context — orientation + active step state
StepperContext: { value: number; onValueChange: (v: number) => void; orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" }

// Per-item context — step index + disabled flag
StepperItemContext: { step: number; disabled: boolean }
```

### Controlled/Uncontrolled pattern

```typescript
function Stepper({ value: valueProp, defaultValue = 1, onValueChange, orientation = "horizontal", ... }) {
  const [uncontrolled, setUncontrolled] = React.useState(defaultValue)
  const active = valueProp ?? uncontrolled
  const handleChange = (step: number) => {
    if (valueProp === undefined) setUncontrolled(step)
    onValueChange?.(step)
  }
  // Provide context + render <div data-slot="stepper" data-orientation={orientation}>
}
```

### Sub-components summary

```typescript
// StepperItem — reads orientation via CSS group, sets item context
// Props: step: number, disabled?: boolean
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-item" data-step={step} data-active={active===step}
//               data-completed={active>step} data-disabled={disabled}>
//   data-orientation flows down from group/stepper via CSS

// StepperTrigger — reads step+disabled from StepperItemContext
// Renders: <button data-slot="stepper-trigger" disabled={disabled}
//              onClick={() => !disabled && onValueChange(step)}>

// StepperTitle
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-title">

// StepperDescription
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-description">

// StepperSeparator
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-separator" aria-hidden>
//   Orientation-aware via group-data-horizontal/stepper vs group-data-vertical/stepper CSS

// StepperContent — reads step from StepperItemContext (falls back to explicit prop)
// Only renders children when step === active
// Props: step?: number
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-content" hidden={!isActive}>
```

### Full className scheme (follows `cn-` prefix convention)

```
Stepper:          "cn-stepper group/stepper flex flex-col"
StepperItem:      "cn-stepper-item group/stepper-item flex items-center
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-row group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-col"
StepperTrigger:   "cn-stepper-trigger flex cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 rounded-sm
                   text-left transition-colors outline-none
                   focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2
                   disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50"
StepperTitle:     "cn-stepper-title text-sm font-medium leading-none"
StepperDescription:"cn-stepper-description text-sm text-muted-foreground"
StepperSeparator: "cn-stepper-separator shrink-0 bg-border
                   group-data-horizontal/stepper:h-px group-data-horizontal/stepper:w-full group-data-horizontal/stepper:flex-1
                   group-data-vertical/stepper:h-full group-data-vertical/stepper:w-px group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-1 group-data-vertical/stepper:self-stretch"
StepperContent:   "cn-stepper-content"  (hidden when not active)
```

### Exports

```typescript
export {
  Stepper,
  StepperItem,
  StepperTrigger,
  StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription,
  StepperSeparator,
  StepperContent,
}
```

---

## Step 2 — `stepper-example.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx`

The radix variant is identical except all import paths change from `bases/base` to `bases/radix`.

The file exports a default `StepperExample` function wrapping all sub-examples in `<ExampleWrapper>`.

### Sub-examples (each in its own named function)

| Function | Demonstrates |
|----------|-------------|
| `StepperBasic` | Horizontal, uncontrolled, 3 steps with titles only |
| `StepperWithDescriptions` | Horizontal, with title + description per step |
| `StepperVertical` | `orientation="vertical"` with `StepperContent` panels inside each item |
| `StepperControlled` | `"use client"`, `useState`, controlled `value`/`onValueChange`, Next/Back buttons |
| `StepperWithDisabled` | One step marked `disabled` |

### Import pattern (follows pagination-example.tsx conventions)
```typescript
"use client"   // at file top — needed for useState in StepperControlled

import { Example, ExampleWrapper } from "@/registry/bases/base/components/example"
import {
  Stepper, StepperItem, StepperTrigger, StepperTitle,
  StepperDescription, StepperSeparator, StepperContent,
} from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper"
import { Button } from "@/registry/bases/base/ui/button"
```

---

## Step 3 — Registry entry for `stepper` UI component

Append to the end of the `ui` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper",
    type: "registry:ui",
    files: [
      {
        path: "ui/stepper.tsx",
        type: "registry:ui",
      },
    ],
    meta: {
      links: {
        docs: "https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/base/stepper",
        examples:
          "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/shadcn-ui/ui/refs/heads/main/apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx",
      },
    },
  },
```

For the **radix** `_registry.ts`, change the URL paths from `base` → `radix`.

---

## Step 4 — Registry entry for `stepper-example`

Append to the end of the `examples` array (before the closing `]`) in **both** example registry files:

```typescript
  {
    name: "stepper-example",
    title: "Stepper",
    type: "registry:example",
    registryDependencies: ["stepper", "button", "example"],
    files: [
      {
        path: "examples/stepper-example.tsx",
        type: "registry:example",
      },
    ],
  },
```

---

## Key Patterns to Follow (cross-check before implementation)

- `"use client"` at the top of all component and example files
- All sub-components use `data-slot="stepper-*"` attributes
- All `className` props start with a `cn-*` semantic class then Tailwind utilities, merged via `cn()`
- Props are typed as `React.ComponentProps<"element"> & { extra props }` — no external type imports needed
- Orientation forwarded via `data-orientation` on root and consumed in children via `group-data-horizontal/stepper:*` / `group-data-vertical/stepper:*` CSS selectors
- Both `base` and `radix` variants get identical implementation (only import path differs: `bases/base` vs `bases/radix`)
- Example file naming: `stepper-example.tsx` (not `stepper-demo.tsx`)
- Example sub-functions are not exported (only the default export is)
- No modifications to any existing files other than appending to the four `_registry.ts` arrays

---

## Verification

1. **TypeScript** — Run `pnpm --filter @v4/app typecheck` (or equivalent) and confirm zero new errors
2. **Visual** — Open the dev server (`pnpm dev` in `apps/v4`) and navigate to the stepper example page to verify all 5 sub-examples render correctly in both horizontal and vertical orientations
3. **Registry validation** — Run `pnpm --filter @v4/app validate-registries` (or `tsx scripts/validate-registries.mts`) to confirm the new entries parse cleanly against the Zod schema
4. **Manual interaction** — Click each `StepperTrigger` in the Controlled example and verify active step advances; verify disabled step cannot be clicked

## Step 1 — `stepper.tsx` (base variant)

**Path:** `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx`

The radix variant (`apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx`) is **identical** except the `cn` import path changes from `@/registry/bases/base/lib/utils` to `@/registry/bases/radix/lib/utils`.

### Architecture

Two React Contexts:

```typescript
// Root context — orientation + active step state
StepperContext: { value: number; onValueChange: (v: number) => void; orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" }

// Per-item context — step index + disabled flag
StepperItemContext: { step: number; disabled: boolean }
```

### Controlled/Uncontrolled pattern

```typescript
function Stepper({ value: valueProp, defaultValue = 1, onValueChange, orientation = "horizontal", ... }) {
  const [uncontrolled, setUncontrolled] = React.useState(defaultValue)
  const active = valueProp ?? uncontrolled
  const handleChange = (step: number) => {
    if (valueProp === undefined) setUncontrolled(step)
    onValueChange?.(step)
  }
  // Provide context + render <div data-slot="stepper" data-orientation={orientation}>
}
```

### Sub-components summary

```typescript
// StepperItem — reads orientation via CSS group, sets item context
// Props: step: number, disabled?: boolean
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-item" data-step={step} data-active={active===step}
//               data-completed={active>step} data-disabled={disabled}>
//   data-orientation flows down from group/stepper via CSS

// StepperTrigger — reads step+disabled from StepperItemContext
// Renders: <button data-slot="stepper-trigger" disabled={disabled}
//              onClick={() => !disabled && onValueChange(step)}>

// StepperTitle
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-title">

// StepperDescription
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-description">

// StepperSeparator
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-separator" aria-hidden>
//   Orientation-aware via group-data-horizontal/stepper vs group-data-vertical/stepper CSS

// StepperContent — reads step from StepperItemContext (falls back to explicit prop)
// Only renders children when step === active
// Props: step?: number
// Renders: <div data-slot="stepper-content" hidden={!isActive}>
```

# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component

## Context

The codebase is a shadcn/ui v4 monorepo (`apps/v4/`) with two parallel base variants — `base` (built on `@base-ui/react`) and `radix` (built on `radix-ui`). Every UI component and example must be added to **both** variants. Neither `@base-ui/react` nor `radix-ui` exposes a native Stepper primitive, so this component is built entirely with React Context and plain HTML elements — the same approach used by `pagination.tsx`.

The component must include:
- `Stepper` — root container with orientation + controlled/uncontrolled active-step state via Context
- `StepperItem` — wraps one step; propagates its `step` number and `disabled` flag via a second nested Context
- `StepperTrigger` — `<button>` that fires `onValueChange(step)`; reads step from `StepperItemContext`
- `StepperTitle` — styled heading text inside a trigger
- `StepperDescription` — styled secondary text inside a trigger
- `StepperSeparator` — visual line between steps; adapts direction via parent orientation data-attribute
- `StepperContent` — panel shown only when its step is active; step inferred from `StepperItemContext` or overridden by explicit `step` prop

content.text:
All diffs are exactly as expected — only import paths differ between variants. Here's a summary of everything that was created and modified:

---

## Implementation Complete

### New files created (4)

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/stepper.tsx` | Base variant — 279 lines |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/stepper.tsx` | Radix variant — identical, only `cn` import differs |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Base demo — 272 lines |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/stepper-example.tsx` | Radix demo — identical, only import paths differ |

### Registry files modified (4, append-only)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/ui/_registry.ts` | Added `stepper` UI entry |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/ui/_registry.ts` | Added `stepper` UI entry |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/base/examples/_registry.ts` | Added `stepper-example` entry |
| `apps/v4/registry/bases/radix/examples/_registry.ts` | Added `stepper-example` entry |

---

### What was built

**`stepper.tsx`** — Compound component with 7 exports:
- **`Stepper`** — Root `<div>` with `data-orientation`, controlled/uncontrolled active-step state managed via `React.useState` + `React.useCallback`, shared via `StepperContext`
- **`StepperItem`** — Per-step wrapper; computes `data-active` / `data-completed` / `data-disabled` from context; exposes step + disabled to descendants via `StepperItemContext`; `flex-col` in vertical mode via `group-data-vertical/stepper:flex-col`
- **`StepperTrigger`** — `<button>` that calls `onValueChange(step)` on click; respects `disabled`; full keyboard focus-ring styles
- **`StepperTitle`** — Styled `<div data-slot="stepper-title">`
- **`StepperDescription`** — Styled `<div data-slot="stepper-description">` in muted text
- **`StepperSeparator`** — Orientation-aware divider: `h-px flex-1` (horizontal) or `h-6 w-px` (vertical) via CSS group selectors
- **`StepperContent`** — Panel with `hidden={!isActive}`; `step` prop optional when nested inside `StepperItem` (inferred from context)

**`stepper-example.tsx`** — Five sub-examples: Basic, With Descriptions, Vertical (content panels inside each item), Controlled (useState + Back/Next buttons), With Disabled Step.
